description: Replace monolithic hand-authored dungeon frames with a compositional generator. Base corridor grids (both walls) are modified by opening modifiers (near/far, left/right) that remove the correct wall boundary characters. Depth tinting and regionColoredLine are unchanged.

steps.name: Base corridor grid builder
steps.description: Define corridor geometry constants and a baseCorridorGrid(depth:) function that returns a mutable [[Character]] grid (58 cols x 15 rows) with both walls intact. Must reproduce the structural skeleton of the existing _none() frames for all 4 depths. Wire into buildFrameTable() for the no-opening cases (nearLeft=false, nearRight=false, farLeft=false, farRight=false) for all depths.
acceptance_criteria: baseCorridorGrid(depth:0) row 0 has | at col 0 and col 57
baseCorridorGrid(depth:3) row 5 contains fog dots (·)
All base grids are exactly 15 rows x 58 chars
buildFrameTable() still returns all four depth=N no-opening keys
Existing DungeonFrameWidthTests and RendererGraphicsPassTests still pass
steps.implementation_notes: Geometry constants from docs/architecture/frame-rendering-design.md: D=0 walls col 0/57, D=1 walls col 3/54 ceiling row 2 floor row 10, D=2 walls col 6/51 ceiling row 4 floor row 8. Perspective chars: D0->D1 ceiling col1=\ col2=\ rows 1-2, right col56=/ col55=/ rows 1-2. Keep _none() functions as helpers initially — they can be inlined or removed in cleanup.

steps.name: Near opening modifier
steps.description: Implement applyNearOpening(_:side:) that removes the D=0 wall boundary on the specified side. Near-right: clear col 57 (|) for all rows 0-12, col 56 (/) row 1 and (\) row 11, col 55 (/) row 2 and (\) row 10. Near-left: mirror (cols 0, 1, 2). Wire all near-opening combinations into buildFrameTable() for all 4 depths. Remove old monolithic nearLeft/nearRight frame functions.
acceptance_criteria: Near-right frame: col 57 is space for rows 0-12
Near-right frame: col 55 row 2 is space (D0->D1 ceiling perspective removed)
Near-right frame: col 54 row 3 is | (D=1 wall intact through gap)
Near-left frame: col 0 is space for rows 0-12 (mirror)
All 4 depths x 4 near-opening variants (nearLeft, nearRight, nearBoth, none) present in buildFrameTable()
All frames still 58 chars wide
All existing tests still pass
steps.implementation_notes: applyNearOpening mutates the [[Character]] grid in-place. Right side: rows 0-12 col 57 = space; row 1 col 56 = space; row 2 col 55 = space; row 10 col 55 = space; row 11 col 56 = space. Left side mirrors: rows 0-12 col 0 = space; row 1 col 1 = space; row 2 col 2 = space; row 10 col 2 = space; row 11 col 1 = space. The D=1 right wall at col 54 (rows 3-9) must NOT be touched.

steps.name: Far opening modifier and fallback cleanup
steps.description: Implement applyFarOpening(_:side:) that removes the D=1 wall boundary on the specified side for rows 4-8 only (the opening zone between D=2 ceiling and D=2 floor). Far-right: clear col 54 (|) rows 4-8, col 52 (/ row 4, \ row 8). Far-left: mirror (col 3 rows 4-8, col 5 row 4/8). Wire all far-opening combinations into buildFrameTable() for depths 1-3. Update fallbackFrame(for:) to no longer strip farLeft/farRight since all combinations are now present.
acceptance_criteria: Far-right frame depth=3: col 54 row 5 is space
Far-right frame depth=3: col 54 row 3 is | (lintel above opening intact)
Far-right frame depth=3: col 54 row 9 is | (lintel below opening intact)
Far-right frame depth=3: col 57 row 5 is | (D=0 outer wall intact through gap)
Far-left frame: mirrors far-right on col 3/5 side
buildFrameTable() contains far-opening keys for depths 1-3
fallbackFrame(for:) no longer strips farLeft/farRight flags
All frames still 58 chars wide
All existing tests still pass
steps.implementation_notes: applyFarOpening mutates grid in-place for rows 4-8 only. Far-right: row 4 col 52 = space (was /), col 53 already space, col 54 = space (was |). Rows 5-7: col 52-53 already space, col 54 = space. Row 8: col 52 = space (was \), col 54 = space. Far-left mirror: row 4 col 5 = space (was \), col 3 = space; rows 5-7 col 3 = space; row 8 col 5 = space, col 3 = space. Depth 0 has no D=1 boundary, skip far openings for depth=0. Update fallbackFrame: remove the candidate step that strips farLeft/farRight to just false — all combinations are now in the table.
